en.extract:
The mangrove whistler (Pachycephala cinerea) is a species of bird in the family Pachycephalidae found in South-east Asia.


Taxonomy and systematics
Alternate names for the mangrove whistler include the grey thickhead and white-bellied whistler. The latter name should not be confused with the species of the same name, Pachycephala leucogastra. Formerly, both the green-backed and white-vented whistlers were considered to be subspecies of the mangrove whistler.


Subspecies
Two subspecies are recognized:

P. c. cinerea – (Blyth, 1847): Found from northeastern India to Indochina and the Greater Sundas
Palawan thickhead (P. c. plateni), or Palawan whistler – (Blasius, W, 1888): Originally described as a separate species. Found in Palawan (Philippines)

The '''mangrove whistler''' ('''''Pachycephala cinerea''''') is a species of [[bird]] in the family [[Pachycephalidae]] found in South-east Asia.

==Taxonomy and systematics==
Alternate names for the mangrove whistler include the '''grey thickhead''' and '''white-bellied whistler'''. The latter name should not be confused with the species of the same name, ''[[Pachycephala leucogastra]]''. Formerly, both the [[Green-backed whistler|green-backed]] and [[white-vented whistler]]s were considered to be subspecies of the mangrove whistler.
[[File:Mangrove Whistler Sundarban West Bengal India August 2019.jpg|thumb|left|''P. c. cinerea'' from [[Sundarbans National Park|Sundarban]], [[India]].]]
===Subspecies===
Two subspecies are recognized:<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.worldbirdnames.org/bow/whistlers/|title=Whiteheads, sitellas & whistlers « IOC World Bird List|website=www.worldbirdnames.org|language=en-US|access-date=2017-01-31}}</ref>
* ''P. c. cinerea'' – <small>([[Edward Blyth|Blyth]], 1847)</small>: Found from northeastern India to [[Indochina]] and the [[Greater Sundas]]
* '''Palawan thickhead''' (''P. c. plateni''), or '''Palawan whistler''' – <small>([[August Wilhelm Heinrich Blasius|Blasius, W]], 1888)</small>: Originally described as a separate species. Found in [[Palawan]] (Philippines)
